summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd discusses her return as CEO of Bumble after a 14-month hiatus. She reflects on the burnout she experienced after a decade of continuous work, likening her connection to the company to a long-term relationship that required a break.

principles.statement: Founders must recognize when it is time to take a break from their company to recover from burnout.
principles.rationale: Working continuously for a decade can lead to severe burnout, requiring a step back to recharge.
principles.source_quote: I had been going for about 10 years straight. I’d been in this long-term relationship with Bumble, and it was time for a break.

mental_models.name: Company as a Long-Term Relationship
mental_models.description: Viewing a founder's dedication and connection to their company as a long-term relationship, which, like any intense partnership, might require a temporary break to sustain healthily.
mental_models.example: She described her 10-year stint running Bumble as a 'long-term relationship' that she needed a break from due to burnout.

signature_quotes.text: I’d been in this long-term relationship with Bumble, and it was time for a break.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining her decision to step down as CEO of Bumble for 14 months.

signature_quotes.text: I had been going for about 10 years straight.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the continuous work that led to her burnout.
